name: Gross Chess
author: Fergus Duniho (2009) / AGP port
description: Fergus Duniho's 'natural extension of Chess' to a 12x12 board of 144 squares (a gross). Each side fields the full chess army plus tried-and-tested pieces from other variants: the Marshall (rook+knight) and Archbishop (bishop+knight), the Omega Chess Champion (leaps 1 orthogonally or 2 in any direction) and Wizard (ferz+camel), and the Chinese-style Cannon and Vao (move like rook/bishop, capture by hopping over one screen). Pawns may open with a two- or three-square step and can be captured en passant on any square they pass; they promote on the last three ranks, only to pieces held in reserve, with the strongest pieces reserved for the last rank. Castling is flexible: the king slides two or more squares toward a rook and the rook leaps to the square just behind it. Checkmate wins; stalemate is a draw.
